project.name: Sales Analytics Dashboard
project.description: Complete data analysis pipeline: ETL, exploratory analysis, statistical tests, visualizations, and executive dashboard using Python, Pandas, Plotly, and Streamlit
project.author: Data Analyst
project.business_objective: Identify top-performing products, customer segments, and sales trends to optimize inventory and marketing strategy

dataset.description: Synthetic sales dataset with 100K records spanning 3 years

pipeline_steps.title: ETL Pipeline
pipeline_steps.code:
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
from datetime import datetime
import logging


def etl_pipeline(input_path: str, output_path: str) -> None:
    """Complete ETL pipeline for sales data."""
    
    # Extract
    logging.info('📥 Loading raw data...')
    df = pd.read_csv(input_path)
    
    # Transform
    df['order_date'] = pd.to_datetime(df['order_date'])
    df['month_year'] = df['order_date'].dt.to_period('M')
    df['week_of_year'] = df['order_date'].dt.isocalendar().week
    df['customer_lifetime_value'] = df.groupby('customer_id')['total_amount'].cumsum()
    
    # Clean
    df = df.dropna()
    df = df[df['quantity'] > 0]
    df = df[df['unit_price'] > 0]
    
    # Feature engineering
    df['profit_margin'] = np.random.uniform(0.1, 0.4, len(df))  # Simulated
    df['gross_profit'] = df['total_amount'] * df['profit_margin']
    
    # Validate
    assert df['total_amount'].sum() > 0, 'No valid sales found'
    
    # Save
    df.to_csv(output_path, index=False)
    logging.info(f'✅ ETL complete: {len(df):,} records processed')
    
    return df

# Usage
processed_df = etl_pipeline('data/raw/sales_data.csv', 'data/processed/sales_clean.csv')

pipeline_steps.title: Exploratory Data Analysis
pipeline_steps.code:
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
from scipy import stats
import sweetviz as sv


def exploratory_analysis(df: pd.DataFrame) -> dict:
    """Generate comprehensive EDA report."""
    insights = {}
    
    # Basic stats
    insights['total_revenue'] = df['total_amount'].sum()
    insights['total_orders'] = len(df)
    insights['avg_order_value'] = df['total_amount'].mean()
    insights['total_customers'] = df['customer_id'].nunique()
    
    # Top categories
    top_categories = (
        df.groupby('product_category')['total_amount']
        .sum()
        .sort_values(ascending=False)
        .round(2)
    )
    insights['top_category'] = top_categories.iloc[0].to_dict()
    
    # Monthly trends
    monthly_sales = df.groupby('month_year')['total_amount'].sum()
    growth_rate = monthly_sales.pct_change().mean() * 100
    insights['monthly_growth_rate'] = f'{growth_rate:.1f}%'
    
    # Statistical tests
    electronics_sales = df[df['product_category'] == 'Electronics']['total_amount']
    clothing_sales = df[df['product_category'] == 'Clothing']['total_amount']
    
    t_stat, p_value = stats.ttest_ind(electronics_sales, clothing_sales)
    insights['electronics_vs_clothing_pvalue'] = p_value
    
    # Customer analysis
    customer_stats = df.groupby('customer_id').agg({
        'total_amount': ['count', 'sum', 'mean'],
        'quantity': 'sum'
    }).round(2)
    insights['top_customer'] = customer_stats['total_amount']['sum'].idxmax()
    
    # Generate Sweetviz report
    report = sv.analyze(df)
    report_html = 'reports/sweetviz_report.html'
    report.save_html(report_html)
    
    return insights

# Usage
df = pd.read_csv('data/processed/sales_clean.csv')
insights = exploratory_analysis(df)
print(insights)

pipeline_steps.title: Advanced Visualizations
pipeline_steps.code:
import plotly.express as px
import plotly.graph_objects as go
import plotly.figure_factory as ff
from plotly.subplots import make_subplots
import pandas as pd


def create_visualizations(df: pd.DataFrame) -> None:
    """Generate comprehensive dashboard visualizations."""
    
    # 1. Sales trend
    monthly_df = df.groupby(['month_year', 'product_category'])['total_amount'].sum().reset_index()
    fig1 = px.line(monthly_df, 
                   x='month_year', y='total_amount', 
                   color='product_category',
                   title='Monthly Sales Trend by Category')
    fig1.update_xaxes(type='category')
    fig1.write_html('reports/sales_trend.html')
    
    # 2. Top products pie chart
    top_products = df.groupby('product_name')['total_amount'].sum().nlargest(10)
    fig2 = px.pie(values=top_products.values, names=top_products.index,
                  title='Top 10 Products by Revenue')
    fig2.write_html('reports/top_products.html')
    
    # 3. Regional heatmap
    region_sales = df.groupby(['region', 'product_category'])['total_amount'].sum().unstack().fillna(0)
    fig3 = px.imshow(region_sales, aspect='auto', color_continuous_scale='Viridis',
                     title='Sales Heatmap: Region vs Category')
    fig3.write_html('reports/regional_heatmap.html')
    
    # 4. Distribution + box plot
    fig4 = make_subplots(
        rows=2, cols=2,
        subplot_titles=('Order Value Distribution', 'Quantity Box Plot',
                       'Sales by Region', 'Category Revenue')
    )
    
    # Distribution
    fig4.add_trace(
        go.Histogram(x=df['total_amount'], name='Order Value', nbinsx=50),
        row=1, col=1
    )
    
    # Box plot
    fig4.add_trace(
        go.Box(y=df['quantity'], name='Quantity', boxpoints='outliers'),
        row=1, col=2
    )
    
    # Regional bar
    region_total = df.groupby('region')['total_amount'].sum()
    fig4.add_trace(
        go.Bar(x=region_total.index, y=region_total.values, name='Region Sales'),
        row=2, col=1
    )
    
    # Category revenue
    cat_total = df.groupby('product_category')['total_amount'].sum()
    fig4.add_trace(
        go.Bar(x=cat_total.index, y=cat_total.values, name='Category Revenue'),
        row=2, col=2
    )
    
    fig4.update_layout(height=800, showlegend=False)
    fig4.write_html('reports/dashboard_overview.html')
    
    print('📊 All visualizations saved to reports/')

# Usage
df = pd.read_csv('data/processed/sales_clean.csv')
create_visualizations(df)

pipeline_steps.title: Interactive Streamlit Dashboard
pipeline_steps.description: Executive dashboard with filters and metrics
pipeline_steps.code:
import streamlit as st
import pandas as pd
import plotly.express as px
from src.analysis import exploratory_analysis
from src.viz import create_visualizations

st.set_page_config(page_title='Sales Analytics', layout='wide')

@st.cache_data
def load_data():
    return pd.read_csv('data/processed/sales_clean.csv')

st.title('🏪 Sales Analytics Dashboard')
st.markdown('---')

# Sidebar filters
col1, col2, col3 = st.columns(3)
with col1:
    region = st.multiselect('Region', ['North', 'South', 'East', 'West'], default=['North', 'South', 'East', 'West'])
with col2:
    category = st.multiselect('Category', ['Electronics', 'Clothing', 'Home', 'Books'], default=['Electronics', 'Clothing', 'Home', 'Books'])
with col3:
    start_date = st.date_input('Start Date', value=pd.to_datetime('2023-01-01'))

# Load & filter data
@st.cache_data
def get_filtered_data(df, region, category, start_date):
    filtered = df[
        (df['region'].isin(region)) &
        (df['product_category'].isin(category)) &
        (df['order_date'] >= pd.to_datetime(start_date))
    ]
    return filtered

# Main dashboard
if 'df' not in st.session_state:
    st.session_state.df = load_data()

filtered_df = get_filtered_data(st.session_state.df, region, category, start_date)

# KPIs
col1, col2, col3, col4 = st.columns(4)

with col1:
    st.metric('Total Revenue', f'${filtered_df["total_amount"].sum():,.0f}')
with col2:
    st.metric('Total Orders', f'{len(filtered_df):,}')
with col3:
    st.metric('Avg Order Value', f'${filtered_df["total_amount"].mean():.0f}')
with col4:
    st.metric('Unique Customers', f'{filtered_df["customer_id"].nunique():,}')

# Charts
col1, col2 = st.columns(2)

with col1:
    # Monthly trend
    monthly = filtered_df.groupby(filtered_df['order_date'].dt.to_period('M'))['total_amount'].sum().reset_index()
    monthly['month'] = monthly['order_date'].astype(str)
    fig1 = px.line(monthly, x='month', y='total_amount', 
                   title='Monthly Sales Trend')
    st.plotly_chart(fig1, use_container_width=True)

with col2:
    # Category breakdown
    cat_sales = filtered_df.groupby('product_category')['total_amount'].sum()
    fig2 = px.pie(values=cat_sales.values, names=cat_sales.index,
                  title='Revenue by Category')
    st.plotly_chart(fig2, use_container_width=True)

# Insights report
if st.button('🔍 Generate Insights Report'):
    with st.spinner('Analyzing...'):
        insights = exploratory_analysis(filtered_df)
        st.json(insights)

st.markdown('---')
st.caption('👨‍💼 Sales Analytics Dashboard v3.0.0')

quick_start.setup: pip install -r requirements.txt && python scripts/generate_data.py && python src/etl.py
quick_start.Makefile:
.PHONY: install data etl analyze dashboard clean

install:
	pip install -r requirements.txt

data:
	python scripts/generate_data.py --records 100000

etl:
	python src/etl.py

analyze:
	python src/etl.py && python src/analysis.py && python src/viz.py

dashboard:
	streamlit run dashboard/app.py

clean:
	rm -rf data/processed/ reports/ *.html

deployment.streamlit_cloud: Connect GitHub repo → Deploy → Share dashboard URL
deployment.docker: docker build -t sales-dashboard . && docker run -p 8501:8501 sales-dashboard
